exercises.title: Contact Database with Multiple Structures
exercises.description: Build a mini contact database that demonstrates the right structure for each need: a list for ordered storage, a dict for fast name lookup, and a set for tag-based search.
exercises.starterCode:
from collections import defaultdict

# Contact storage: list of dicts (ordered records)
contacts = [
    {"name": "Alice Johnson", "email": "alice@techco.com", "tags": {"python", "developer"}},
    {"name": "Bob Smith", "email": "bob@startup.io", "tags": {"javascript", "developer"}},
    {"name": "Carol Williams", "email": "carol@techco.com", "tags": {"python", "data-science"}},
    {"name": "David Brown", "email": "david@freelance.net", "tags": {"python", "freelance"}},
]

# TODO: Build a name_index dict mapping lowercase name to contact dict
# (for fast O(1) lookup)

# TODO: Build a tag_index defaultdict(list) mapping each tag to list of names

# TODO: Print all Python developers (names only, sorted)
# Format: "Python developers: ['Alice Johnson', 'Carol Williams', 'David Brown']"

# TODO: Look up 'bob smith' in name_index and print their email
# Format: "Bob Smith: bob@startup.io"

# TODO: Print total unique tags across all contacts
# Format: "Unique tags: 5"
exercises.expectedOutput:
Python developers: ['Alice Johnson', 'Carol Williams', 'David Brown']
Bob Smith: bob@startup.io
Unique tags: 5
hints: name_index = {c['name'].lower(): c for c in contacts}
For tag_index: for c in contacts: for tag in c['tags']: tag_index[tag].append(c['name'])
All unique tags: set of all tags across all contacts

exercises.title: Data Pipeline with All Structures
exercises.description: Process a dataset of student grades using lists, dicts, sets, Counter, and generators together. Compute per-student averages, find the honor roll (avg >= 85), and get top 3 subjects by class average.
exercises.starterCode:
from collections import Counter

students = [
    {"name": "Alice", "grades": {"Math": 92, "Science": 88, "English": 95, "History": 87}},
    {"name": "Bob", "grades": {"Math": 75, "Science": 82, "English": 68, "History": 71}},
    {"name": "Carol", "grades": {"Math": 98, "Science": 91, "English": 89, "History": 94}},
    {"name": "David", "grades": {"Math": 65, "Science": 70, "English": 88, "History": 72}},
]

# TODO: Calculate average grade for each student
# Print each: "Alice: 90.50"

print()

# TODO: Print names of students on the honor roll (average >= 85), sorted
# Format: "Honor roll: ['Alice', 'Carol']"

print()

# TODO: Calculate the class average for each subject, then print the top 3
# Format: "Top subjects: [('Math', 82.5), ('Science', 82.75), ('English', 85.0)]"
# Sort by average descending, take top 3
exercises.expectedOutput:
Alice: 90.50
Bob: 74.00
Carol: 93.00
David: 73.75

Honor roll: ['Alice', 'Carol']

Top subjects: [('English', 85.0), ('Carol', 93.0), ('Science', 82.75)]
hints: avg = sum(s['grades'].values()) / len(s['grades']) for each student
Honor roll: [s['name'] for s in students if avg >= 85]
Build subject_totals dict, then sort by value descending
For subject averages: iterate all students and accumulate per subject

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Course 5 Summary
warmup.description: This warmup reviews all Course 5 data structures. What does each line print?
warmup.starterCode:
from collections import Counter, defaultdict, deque

# List
data = [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6]
print(sorted(data)[:3])

# Set
print(len(set(data)))

# Dict
d = {x: x**2 for x in range(1, 5)}
print(d)

# Counter
print(Counter(data).most_common(2))
